rows.notes: Top-of-page title in Latin. Handwriting mostly legible though the ordinal/abbreviation for the day is slightly faded; no monetary columns present on this line.

Pounds (239) and shillings (0) align with the leftmost and middle ruling; pence reads as '8' with a trailing loop interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). Ink is slightly faint here which makes the fraction reading moderately uncertain.
Numbers clearly aligned under the three ruling columns. Pence shows a small tail/mark consistent with a farthing (1/4 = 0.25). Description abbreviated but legible as 'Rec: Anni presentis'.
Pounds/shillings clear. Pence column shows '0' with a small loop/ob mark indicating a halfpenny (0.5). The loop is faint, so fraction reading is moderately uncertain.
Clear handwriting and crisp numerals. No fractional mark after the pence; values pass currency validation (shillings <20, pence <12).
Clearly aligned subtotal line. Pence shows a small mark interpreted as a farthing (0.25). Vertical alignment with the three ruling lines is unambiguous.
Section header centered/indented; no monetary values on this line.
Description and amounts are clear. No fractional pence. All numerals well-formed and align under the ruling lines.

rows.description: Schol: Car: mi Reg: et schol: (vacans?)
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings/pence are legible (926/19/4). The description is abbreviated and partly faded; parenthetical '(vacans?)' indicates uncertainty in a word following the abbreviation 'schol:'. Numerals align well with the ruling lines.

A short subline under the scholorships shows 13 pounds, 14 shillings, 1 pence with a small farthing mark (0.25). This line is cramped and a bit faded — the textual label is unclear so marked as '(sub‑item)'.

The 'Varia' label is legible. Amount reads 1157 8 11 1/2 (pence 11 with a halfpenny mark). Numerals are darker here and align with the column guide; minor smudge on the fraction mark makes fraction reading slightly uncertain but likely a halfpenny.
Subtotal line clearly written and aligned. Pence shows the visual of three‑quarters (3q) — interpreted as 0.75. All values within valid ranges.
This repeats the receipts subtotal as a carry‑line. Pence looks like '4' with a trailing loop interpreted as a halfpenny (0.5) though the loop is light — moderate confidence.
Amounts read as 1009/9/9 with a faint halfpenny mark after the pence. Alignment with ruling lines is clear; fraction reading is slightly uncertain due to light ink.
This computed total is clearly written as 2919/16/1 3/4. The pence fraction is visually three‑quarters (0.75). Columns line up well.
Label indicates deduction of the sums paid. Amount matches the earlier 'Summa Solut' (2214/10/11 3/4). Clear alignment and 3/4 pence mark evident.
Final balance line at right-most column reads 704/17/2. No fractional pence visible. Text is slightly abbreviated but intent is clear (final balance owing).
Statement preceding signatures; clear Latin phrase and no monetary figures.
Date line is legible; no amounts.
Signature line: 'Tho:' abbreviation and surname readable though flourishing handwriting reduces certainty on exact spelling of surname; no amount.
Signature appears to be 'J. Cole' with a title approximating 'Subrectorem' or 'Sub‑rectorem'. Handwriting of title is faint and abbreviated.
Name and title 'Decanum' are reasonably clear; surname reading 'Demainbray' matches letter shapes though older orthography may vary.
Signature is heavily abbreviated and stylised. I read initials 'T.G.' and an abbreviated surname 'Hubb.' followed by 'Bur: Junr.' The exact expansion is uncertain due to flourish and ink thickness.
Signature readable as 'J. Hart' with academic postnominals 'A.M.'; slight ink fade on the surname but fairly clear.
Final signature reads 'J. L. Heyes' with 'A.M.' The surname letters are slightly compressed by the flourish; reading chosen based on letter shapes.
